title: Name Banks — Expanded and Regional
philosophy: Names worn smooth by use. They sound like they have been in mouths for generations. Nothing invented for invention's sake. Drawn from Old English, Norse, Celtic, Frankish, Breton, and medieval Germanic roots — filtered through each region's cultural character. Names should feel like they belong to a place, not like they were chosen from a list.

solvane_amber_coast.character: Warm southern vowels, softer consonants, occasionally Romance-adjacent. The coast's wealth means names have sometimes been polished — shorter rough edges than the northern regions. Surnames often reference geography, water, or old Solvanic family trades.
solvane_amber_coast.avoid: Heavy consonant clusters, strongly Germanic sounds, names ending in hard stops
solvane_amber_coast.feel: Sera, Pelvan, Fenra, Gorvan, Liret, Calla, Tavan

valdenmere_thornsveld.character: Harder consonants, northern weight, Old Norse and Old English adjacent. Names have functional origins — many derive from occupations, landscapes, or old clan identifiers. Surnames often geographic or descriptive.
valdenmere_thornsveld.avoid: Soft southern vowels, Romance-adjacent names, names that feel warm
valdenmere_thornsveld.feel: Halvard, Esven, Torven, Keld, Weld, Brena, Jorn, Soven

ossrath_keth_plateau.character: Dry and clipped. Short names common. Some feel genuinely ancient — worn down to almost nothing by time. Drift Lord names are their own register: older still, sometimes single syllable, sometimes with a quality that doesn't map to any surviving linguistic root.
ossrath_keth_plateau.avoid: Long flowing names, obviously warm or cold associations
ossrath_keth_plateau.feel: Orn, Dara, Kael, Rael, Bren (plateau variant), Drev, Marra, Essa

drenmark_free_fens.character: Soft and wet. Some Celtic-adjacent qualities. Names often have old meanings that residents no longer track — the meaning has worn away and only the sound remains. Surnames frequently reference water, fen features, or old station names.
drenmark_free_fens.avoid: Hard consonant starts, dry plateau sounds
drenmark_free_fens.feel: Vaela, Gorra, Morra, Eddan, Sova, Brynn, Cael, Wren

cauldenmoor_borderlands.character: The contested middle — most varied naming on Arneth because the borderlands have absorbed influence from every direction. Names from any regional register can appear here, but the rougher Germanic-adjacent names are most common among common folk. Merchant and noble names trend toward Solvanic polish.
cauldenmoor_borderlands.feel: Mixed. Aldren, Morra, Halvard, Brenvar, Torven all coexist

shattered_reach.character: Maritime and functional. Short names that work in wind. Many single-syllable surnames. Strong consonants that carry over deck noise. Some names have clearly been borrowed from every port the Reach trades with and have lost their original cultural context.
shattered_reach.feel: Sable, Rael, Fen, Orn (borrowed from plateau), Drel, Tav, Kern

karn_clans.character: Their own register — pastoral, clan-identified. First names are functional and short. The clan name (Ironmark, Longwalker, Dustwalker, Stonecaller, etc.) carries more social weight than the personal name. Personal names often describe a quality or circumstance.
karn_clans.feel: Kael, Drev, Bren, Halvard (borrowed), Orn (overlap with plateau)

drift_lords.character: The oldest names on Arneth. Some have worn down to single or double syllables with no traceable linguistic root. Feel ancient in a way the other registers don't. Clan descriptors (Longwalker, Dustwalker, etc.) are earned or inherited, not chosen.
drift_lords.avoid: Anything that sounds recently coined or culturally specific to settled nations
drift_lords.feel: Orn, Drev, Kessa, Essa, Marra, Tav, Dav, Kern, Hesh, Orvath

ossrath_keth_plateau.character: Often single-syllable or with plateau-specific suffixes (-ath, -eth, -vath)

cauldenmoor_borderlands.character: Most mixed — draws from all regional pools

naming_rules.no_more_than_three_instances: Any given first name should not appear more than three times across all NPCs in all regions. Surnames may repeat within the same region only if the repetition is intentional (same family) or if they are genuinely very common (Keth, Dalt, Crale in Ossrath).
naming_rules.regional_consistency: A character's name should match their region of origin. A Solvanic character named Halvard is unusual and should have a reason for it.
naming_rules.a_name_reduction: The A-name clustering (Aldren, Aldeth, Aldric, Alden) reflects an over-reliance on one starting sound. Going forward, A-names are treated as common Cauldenmoor/Valdenmere names that should not appear more than once per settlement. In Solvane they should be rare. In Ossrath they should be almost absent.
naming_rules.surname_reuse: Common surnames can appear across regions if they are genuinely common (like Smith equivalents). Voss is an Ossrath plateau surname and should not appear on the Amber Coast. Greywood is broadly used but is more common in forested regions.
naming_rules.the_test: Read the name aloud. Does it sound like a real person who has been alive for thirty years? Does it sound like someone's mother would have named them? If yes, it works. If it sounds like it was generated, it doesn't.

corrections_pending.note: These corrections apply to existing files. To be applied in bulk before code implementation.

amber_coast_corrections.reason: Surname collision with Prince Pelvan Calmaveth; A-name reduction
A-name reduction; Liret is appropriately Solvanic
A-name reduction; Davan is appropriately Solvanic
A-name reduction; Cavan is appropriately Solvanic
A-name reduction; Fenra is new and Solvanic
A-name reduction; Voss surname collision with Keth region; Crest is coastal-appropriate
A-name reduction; Crale is kept as it is a common coastal surname
A-name reduction; Rael is short and maritime

keth_corrections.note: Multiple instances of Aldren Keth, Vaela in same region — acceptable as common names in Ossrath per naming rules. No correction needed.

general_notes: Neva Weld (Thornsveld) is the only Neva — no collision, no correction needed
Torven appears in Thornsveld Marches (Torven Weld, Torven Greywood) and Keth (Torven Graywood) — three instances, at the limit, do not add more
Halvard appears in Thornsveld (Halvard Keld, Halvard Ironmark), Keth (Halvard Veld), Amber Coast (Halvard Keld notary, Halvard Greywood) — overused, new Halvards should not be added
Fenwick is used in Thornsveld (Fenwick Grale), Keth (Fenwick Saltfield, Fenwick Caldren), Amber Coast (Fenwick Pale, Fenwick Saltfield advisor) — at limit
Gorvan is used in Keth (Gorvan Arvane) and proposed for Amber Coast Factor-General (Gorvan Delvane) — two instances, acceptable
